title: [cross-impl] Trickle reset guard uses divergent proxies across Rust/Python/C
description: Reviewed last 20 commits (bd auto-updates + a50ae4188 trickle.c alignment). Rust uses state==Stopped || interval!=imin; Python uses _generation==0 || interval!=imin; C uses transmit_time==0 || interval!=imin (post a50ae4188). Same RFC 6206 reset algorithm but different sentinel values risk divergent behavior on time=0, overflow, or init edge cases (see project-LICHEN-67ca). Test vectors in test/vectors/ and rpl tests should be extended for cross-impl determinism. Matches focus on "same algorithm different behavior".
notes: Commit: ada77bf98 - aligned trickle reset guards (interval==0 sentinel in C, > imin in Python, updated comments and tests for cross-impl determinism per RFC 6206 rule 6 and project-LICHEN-67ca)
